The court stated that it received the joint trial plan and asked who will be administering the estate during the stay if Alice is unable to act as the administrator. Mr. Papazian stated that pursuant to the Code he will be acting as the interim petitioner, that he would like the court to stay the matter, and that he would like to be able to proceed with the final accounting to move this along. The court noted that it received statements from First Republic Bank ATM Rebate Checking Account in the name of Alice Anivian ITF Simon Chouldijian Nephew, questions who Simon is and asked for clarification. Mr. Zetjian stated that he would like to whole case to be stayed, that there may be some objections to the accountings depending on the outcome of the Los Angeles County matter, and that he objects to a piece meal stay. Mr. Zetjian also stated that there is a trial in Los Angeles in July to determine what the bylaws are, and that the RICO case is set in August. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, the Petition for Special Letters of Administration, the Fourth Accounting and the trial are continued to September 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Vicken Papazian, Esq. for Alice Anivian (Petitioner). David Rosen, Esq. for the Armenian Revolution Federation WUSA, Inc. (Interested Party). Vatche Zetjian, Esq. for ARF Dashnaksutyan, Western U.S.A. and the Armenian Cultural Foundation (Objectors). Simon Chouldjian also appeared via Zoom.

The Court stated that trial is set for February 8th, that the determination of the proper beneficiary is what is pending, that the parties were ordered to mediation, and that mediation failed. Mr. Papazian stated that the 3rd accounting is pending and that the 4th accounting would be submitted in early January. Mr. Zetjian stated that having the 4th accounting on file will be helpful with regard to any additional objections they wish to raise. Mr. Rosen stated that they have no objection to the 4th accounting being filed in January. The Court stated that it would not vacate the February 7th or 8th dates and that the 4th accounting is to be filed in January. Mr. Papazian addressed the issue regarding the beneficiary. The Court stated that the accountings are separate from the proper beneficiary issue, briefly addressed mediation, inquired whether or not the parties can resolve the issue(s), and stated that the parties appear to be at an impasse. Mr. Rosen stated that it is not a total impasse, that there is a chance that it could settle, that there are many issues, that maybe there could be a global settlement, that the parties know the issues and what can be done, and that the parties need to sit down and work it out. The Court stated that the issue of the personal representative is still pending, that it intends on keeping the pending dates of February 7th and 8th as set, and that the 4th accounting is to be filed by mid-January.

Mr. Rosen requests that the Court consider continuing the trial date of February 8th one month. Brief discussions are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court continued the trial date of February 8, 2023, to March 8, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.

Alice Anivian's petition for probate and ARF Dashnaksutyan, Western U.S.A. and the Armenian Cultural Foundation's petition for special letters of administration are continued to March 8, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Ceclia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.

David Rosen appeared by zoom for Armenian Revolutionary Federation WUSA, Inc.

Mr. Papazian confirmed that the final accounting petition and the petition to appoint a personal representative could go off calendar. Counsel reported that they met and conferred, that they had lengthy conversation and that they did not come to a conclusion. Mr. Zetjian presented oral argument to support the appointment of Mr. Singer. Mr. Papazian presented oral argument to support the appointment of Mr. Chouldjian. Further discussion was held regarding Ms. Anivian's lack of capacity, the issue of there being a vacancy, the appointment of and benefit of having a neutral party appointed, Mr. Chouldjian having been identified in the will to serve, the issue of whether or not ARF is the proper beneficiary, the issue of whether or not there are grounds for removal or the disqualification of Mr. Chouldjian, the value of the estate, the amount each beneficiary is entitled to receive, the requirement of providing notice to any party who has filed a request for special notice, the need to conduct discovery, the length of time needed to be ready for trial (6 months), the length of the trial (2-days), etc. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 18, 2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 22, 2022. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, and the Petition for Special Letters of Administration are continued to and the Parties are to return on November 30, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mr. Zetjian is to monitor the court's register of actions and serve the pro bono mediation order on all parties. The court informed the parties that it will be issuing a case management order, that it will also set a case management hearing for the same date of November 30, 2022, that they will have to submit a joint case management statement and that they should anticipate setting of the trial in late January/early February. Mr. Papzian is to monitor the court's register of actions and serve the case management order on all parties. Mr. Papazian is also to follow up and address the issues raised by the proabte examiner. Status of the accounting is continued to October 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 at Mr. Papazian's request.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vicken Papazian, via Zoom, for Alice Anivian, Personal Representative; Vatche Zetjian, via Zoom, for Armenian Cultural Foundation and ARF Dashnaksutyun, Objectors.

Mr. Papazian reports that the resignation of his client was the main reason that he has failed to respond to the Examiner's notes. The court reminds counsel that he can verify pleadings on his client's behalf regardless off her current position. The court points to the amended accounting filed at the end of February of this year and asks if counsel believes that the accounting will improve. Mr. Papazian advises that he is working on issues he's found with past accountings and is currently searching for a CPA in the San Francisco area who is familiar with this court's process so that he can submit an amended petition. The court acknowledges Mr. Papazian's position but advises that the court is hoping to move forward with the instant petition and have him work with a local CPA on subsequent accounting petitions. Mr. Zetjian offers testimony regarding his position and points to the "Impostor" beneficiary and the Los Angeles Superior Court's ruling regarding elections. The court and counsel discuss the petitions set for hearing in this matter on Aug-08-2022 and confirms with Mr. Zetjian that he is requesting the appointment of a local professional Fiduciary as Administrator. The court encourages the parties to meet and confer regarding the appointment of a successor Administrator and advises that the court is taking a short path with this matter due to the delays in adjudicating this matter and that there will be an evidentiary hearing if the parties are unable to reach an agreement as to the appointment of an Administrator.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vicken Papazian appeared by zoom for and with Alice Anivian by zoom.
Simon Choulejian (Nephew of Alice) also present by zoom.

The Court stated that it is a little bit of a loss as to why this estate is still open. Mr. Papazian stated that it can be attributable to him, that he was advised by the Probate Examiner to obtain assistance with the accounting, that he has contacted almost everyone from the list of paralegals that was provided and that he heard back from a few of them. Mr. Papazian also stated that he will engage someone who is local to assist with the accountings, that he commits to making this estate a priority, that he has spoken with the beneficiaries regarding waiver of the accountings, that there are no objections and that he understands that if he can get waivers, he can close this estate a lot sooner. The Court informed Mr. Papazian that if he can get waivers from all of the beneficiaries, he can file the waivers and amend his petition to request a waiver of the accounting. Mr. Papazian stated that they provided notice to the Attorney General's Office. The Fourth Amended Petition and Account for the Initial Two Years, etc. is under submission. Status on filing of the second accounting for the period of November 1, 2015 through October 31, 2018 (a three-year accounting) is set for April 13, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Papazian is to have his petition on file by April 6, 2021, or have waivers filed by that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
